question.label: Self-read opener
question.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
question.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

answer: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
note: He can identify wins but explicitly says he is unsure whether they meet the team bar, which is a strong clarity gap with mild engagement signal.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: name wins, always — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
note: He says feedback is useful but names a specific recurring failure mode—losing consistency under time pressure—which confirms a strong clarity gap while staying engaged with improving.

question.label: Time pressure consistency
question.name: Under time pressure, what specifically starts to slip in your work?
question.description: Drills into his named failure mode to get concrete evidence of what breaks first.

answer: I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
note: He names a specific clarity failure mode: under pressure he optimizes polish over the core message.

question.label: Recent example
question.name: What is one recent piece of your brand work that best shows your current quality bar?
question.description: Asks for a concrete artifact that reveals how he is actually applying his standards.

answer: I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
note: Asked for a recent example, but he named a missing standard instead — strong clarity gap, with mild disengagement from not owning a concrete example.

question.label: Excellent work examples
question.name: What examples of excellent junior-level work feel missing to you right now?
question.description: Drills into his request for clearer examples so the missing standard becomes concrete.

answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
note: He describes broad reviews leaving him unsure what to change first — strong clarity deficit, with mild strain from not knowing where to focus.

question.label: Broad reviews gap
question.name: When feedback is broad, what part of your work gets missed most often?
question.description: Turns his complaint about broad reviews into a concrete recurring gap in the work.

answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
note: He pivoted from the asked work example to a growth need, signaling strong lack of clarity about what to change while not giving direct wellbeing signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] concept rationale growth thread surfaced but drill cap forces arc advance.

question.label: Missed signal
question.name: Where do you think your work most often misses the mark?
question.description: Names the recurring failure mode without softening it into vague self-critique.

answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
note: Names a missing improvement structure — a tighter loop on one skill at a time — which signals low clarity on how to improve and mild growth blockage.

question.label: Growth blocker
question.name: What is the main thing that is slowing your growth right now?
question.description: Identifies the bottleneck he experiences so the next development focus can be practical.

answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.
note: Names missing alignment on concrete criteria as the growth bottleneck, which is also a mild clarity deficit.

question.label: Support needed
question.name: What would help you make that practice stick this month?
question.description: Gets to the support or constraint that will determine whether the commitment is realistic.

answer: I will start by writing one explicit priority rule I will use when time pressure hits this month.
note: He named a specific practice and trigger — writing an explicit priority rule for time pressure — which is a concrete positive commitment on growth and clarity.
